Title: The 404 page
Summary: Two ways to land on it: bad URL, or a real route with a missing entity.
Body:
## Two trigger paths

The 404 page mounts in two cases:

1. The URL does not match any route. The catch all `<Route path="*">` at the end of the route block routes to `<NotFoundPage />`.
2. The URL matches a route but the entity inside does not exist. `/npcs/eddieioiu` matches `/npcs/:npcId` so the router happily mounts `<NpcEdit />`, but the API returns null. Each entity edit page maps the "fetched null" case to a sentinel and renders `<NotFoundPage />` directly.

## What it shows

A large pixel art "404" rendered in the display font, plus a 24 by 24 LostSignal robot illustration (intact antenna, hollow O eyes, broken signal arcs and a stray "?" pixel mark), plus three navigation links: Go home, Open dialog graph, Diagnostics.

## Distinct from the broken robot

The error boundary's broken robot is RED with sparks and X eyes: something is broken, you need to reload. The 404's LostSignal robot is NEUTRAL with intact antenna and hollow eyes: confused, needs a redirect. Different problem, different visual.
